items.label: Carthage
items.hint: A Phoenician trading city on the North African coast that rivaled Rome.

items.label: Ancient Egypt
items.hint: Civilization along the Nile, unified for millennia around the river's delta.

items.label: Kingdom of Kush
items.hint: A Nubian kingdom south of Egypt that once ruled the Nile Valley as its 25th dynasty.

items.label: Kingdom of Aksum
items.hint: A powerful trading empire in the Horn of Africa, an early adopter of Christianity.

items.label: Mali Empire
items.hint: Ruled from Timbuktu, made fabulously wealthy by gold and the salt trade.

items.label: Great Zimbabwe
items.hint: A stone-walled city that gave modern Zimbabwe its name.
